label: Customer Name
1. Can fill out a paper/online job application completely and correctly or has a responsible party who can assist when necessary (no blank items, signed)
rowLabel: 1. Can fill out a paper/online job application completely and correctly or has a responsible party who can assist when necessary (no blank items, signed)

label: 2. Resume or fact sheet developed. The resume must be submitted to VR with the Vendor Authorization for payment of job readiness services.
rowLabel: 2. Resume or fact sheet developed. The resume must be submitted to VR with the Vendor Authorization for payment of job readiness services.

label: 3. References contacted (by the provider of job readiness services) in advance with complete information on name, address, telephone #, job title. The customer's reference sheet must be submitted to VR with the Vendor Authorization for payment of job readiness services.
rowLabel: 3. References contacted (by the provider of job readiness services) in advance with complete information on name, address, telephone #, job title. The customer's reference sheet must be submitted to VR with the Vendor Authorization for payment of job readiness services.

label: 4. Demonstrates understanding of legal implications of signature on application (drug screen, felony record, etc.)
rowLabel: 4. Demonstrates understanding of legal implications of signature on application (drug screen, felony record, etc.)

label: 5. Drug screening and ability to pass has been discussed with customer.
rowLabel: 5. Drug screening and ability to pass has been discussed with customer.

label: 6. Education section fully completed accurately with dates.
rowLabel: 6. Education section fully completed accurately with dates.

label: 1. Can identify work interests. Understands if they are realistic.
rowLabel: 1. Can identify work interests. Understands if they are realistic.

label: 2. Understands the importance of liking the job and feeling a part of the work environment (keeping the job, working harder, making fewer mistakes).
rowLabel: 2. Understands the importance of liking the job and feeling a part of the work environment (keeping the job, working harder, making fewer mistakes).

label: 3. Understands and can discuss own abilities and aptitudes.
rowLabel: 3. Understands and can discuss own abilities and aptitudes.

label: 4. Understands the importance of motivation, attention and dependability.
rowLabel: 4. Understands the importance of motivation, attention and dependability.

label: 5. Discuss being present every day, being on time, and getting along with others.
rowLabel: 5. Discuss being present every day, being on time, and getting along with others.

label: 6. Can explain the benefits of working.
rowLabel: 6. Can explain the benefits of working.

label: 7. Understands how to perform a job search (Career Centers, friends/family, newspaper, internet, staffing services, staying organized).
rowLabel: 7. Understands how to perform a job search (Career Centers, friends/family, newspaper, internet, staffing services, staying organized).

label: 1. Understands first contact and first impressions.
rowLabel: 1. Understands first contact and first impressions.

label: 2. Can understand potential job interview questions.

rowLabel: 7. Can write out answers or verbally respond to interview questions.

label: 8. Understands the need for follow up after an interview (phone call, thank you note).

label: 9. Can explain what may be perceived as negatives on application (convictions, lapses in employment).

label: 10. Understands pros and cons of disclosure of disability and knows how to request a reasonable accommodation.

label: 1. Contingency plan/barriers to employment (transportation, childcare, illness, weather, gas/lunch money, alarm clock). Ongoing issues must be on the Job Placement Activity Plan.

label: 1. Understands the importance of giving two weeks notice, leaving on good terms, and making sure there is another job to go to.
